hallucinations.description: The paper presents detailed quantitative results (specific Cohen's d values, p-values, tables of means, pooled statistics, etc.) as outcomes of running inference on four reward models. However, the provided experiment log and code show that real model inference failed due to loading errors (e.g., transformers incompatibility with ArmoRM) and the paper itself admits results were produced via simulation matching expected effect size distributions rather than actual model runs.
hallucinations.evidence: Abstract: 'The findings are based on simulated inference due to software compatibility constraints'; Section 3.4: 'experimental results were generated via simulation matching expected effect size distributions from prior literature rather than actual model inference'; experiment.log shows 'ERROR - Failed to load armo' and falls back to stimulus generation only.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations found: the paper fabricates/simulates detailed experimental results and tables while explicitly acknowledging that actual inference was never performed. Severity is high because core claims and all numeric findings rest on this fabrication.
